messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this section of a document.
Focus on what questions this section can answer: topics, entities, dates,
definitions, procedures, claims, tables, figures, metrics, and evidence types.
Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Section metadata:
{
  "node_id": "0104",
  "title": "真正决定全球上线成败的关键是什么？",
  "page_index": 53,
  "page_range": "53-54"
}

Section text:
真正决定全球上线成败的关键是什么？
⚫软启动数据偏差 ：软启竞争力， Beta阶段需达成 45%的D1留存率，方可支撑 30%的全球目标。
⚫10小时规则 ：首周累计游戏时长达 600分钟（ 10小时）的玩家，转化为付费用户的概率提升 5倍。
应抓住 D1或D2的时长高峰，及时触发限时优惠，最大化转化效率。指标 第一天 第七天
总游戏时长 高（60–90 分钟） 可变（取决于活动 / 战争）
会话次数 低（2–3 次长会话） 高（8–12 次短会话）
计时器时长 秒级到分钟级 小时级（ 4–8 小时）
核心驱动因素 即时成长反馈 联盟压力与活动驱动
关键风险计时器过早变长易
导致无聊若在D4 前未加入联盟，
易产生孤立感
corp.funtap.vn首周游戏时长行为分析
53
4X策略游戏：竞争力评分卡
KPI指标 测试阶段 软启动阶段 全球上线
D1留存率 35% 35%-40% >45%
D7留存率 12% 15%-18% >18%
D1游戏时长 >30分钟 >60分钟 >90分钟
会话频率 4次/天 8-10次/天 12+次/天
联盟加入率 (D3) 40% >65% >80%

message.content:
This section answers retrieval queries regarding the critical success factors, behavioral benchmarks, and KPI thresholds for the global launch of 4X strategy games. It specifically addresses:
- **Topics & Entities:** Global launch success determinants, soft launch competitiveness, first-week player engagement, 4X strategy game monetization, alliance onboarding, and limited-time offer optimization.
- **Timeframes & Milestones:** D1, D2, D3, D4, D7, first week, 10-hour (600-minute) threshold, and development phases (Test, Soft Launch, Global Launch).
- **Definitions & Claims:** Defines the “10-hour rule” (600 minutes cumulative playtime in week one increases paying user conversion probability by 5x). Claims that achieving 45% D1 retention in soft launch is required to support a 30% global target. Asserts that D1/D2 duration peaks are optimal for triggering limited-time offers.
- **Procedures & Strategies:** Recommends triggering monetization prompts during D1/D2 playtime peaks. Advises mitigating churn by preventing early timer bloat (causes boredom) and ensuring alliance onboarding before D4 (prevents isolation).
- **Metrics & Benchmarks:** Provides exact KPI targets across Test, Soft Launch, and Global Launch: D1 Retention (35% → 35–40% → >45%), D7 Retention (12% → 15–18% → >18%), D1 Playtime (>30 → >60 → >90 mins), Session Frequency (4 → 8–10 → 12+/day), and D3 Alliance Join Rate (40% → >65% → >80%).
- **Tables & Figures:** Contains two structured data tables: (1) “First-Week Gameplay Duration Behavior Analysis” (sourced from corp.funtap.vn) comparing Day 1 vs. Day 7 metrics for total playtime, session count, timer duration, core drivers, and key risks. (2) “4X Strategy Game: Competitiveness Scorecard” mapping stage-gate KPI thresholds.
- **Evidence Types:** Quantitative performance benchmarks, behavioral analytics, stage-gate progression metrics, and internal corporate data references.

     - "10-hour rule": Players reaching 600 mins in the first week are 5x more likely to convert to paying users.
     - Timing limited-time offers at D1/D2 duration peaks maximizes conversion.
     - Core drivers shift from immediate growth feedback (Day 1) to alliance pressure/events (Day 7).
     - Risks: Timers getting too long too early causes boredom; not joining an alliance by D4 causes isolation.
   - *Procedures/Strategies:* Trigger limited-time offers during D1/D2 duration peaks; ensure alliance joining by D3/D4 to prevent isolation.
   - *Tables/Metrics:*

   - **Tables & Data Sources:** Two main tables: (1) First-week gameplay duration behavior analysis (Day 1 vs. Day 7 metrics, drivers, risks), sourced from corp.funtap.vn. (2) 4X Strategy Game Competitiveness Scorecard detailing KPI thresholds across development stages.

response_text:
This section answers retrieval queries regarding the critical success factors, behavioral benchmarks, and KPI thresholds for the global launch of 4X strategy games. It specifically addresses:
- **Topics & Entities:** Global launch success determinants, soft launch competitiveness, first-week player engagement, 4X strategy game monetization, alliance onboarding, and limited-time offer optimization.
- **Timeframes & Milestones:** D1, D2, D3, D4, D7, first week, 10-hour (600-minute) threshold, and development phases (Test, Soft Launch, Global Launch).
- **Definitions & Claims:** Defines the “10-hour rule” (600 minutes cumulative playtime in week one increases paying user conversion probability by 5x). Claims that achieving 45% D1 retention in soft launch is required to support a 30% global target. Asserts that D1/D2 duration peaks are optimal for triggering limited-time offers.
- **Procedures & Strategies:** Recommends triggering monetization prompts during D1/D2 playtime peaks. Advises mitigating churn by preventing early timer bloat (causes boredom) and ensuring alliance onboarding before D4 (prevents isolation).
- **Metrics & Benchmarks:** Provides exact KPI targets across Test, Soft Launch, and Global Launch: D1 Retention (35% → 35–40% → >45%), D7 Retention (12% → 15–18% → >18%), D1 Playtime (>30 → >60 → >90 mins), Session Frequency (4 → 8–10 → 12+/day), and D3 Alliance Join Rate (40% → >65% → >80%).
- **Tables & Figures:** Contains two structured data tables: (1) “First-Week Gameplay Duration Behavior Analysis” (sourced from corp.funtap.vn) comparing Day 1 vs. Day 7 metrics for total playtime, session count, timer duration, core drivers, and key risks. (2) “4X Strategy Game: Competitiveness Scorecard” mapping stage-gate KPI thresholds.
- **Evidence Types:** Quantitative performance benchmarks, behavioral analytics, stage-gate progression metrics, and internal corporate data references.
